description: Quiet, healthy week. One shipped feature, a few thoughtful reviews, a small incident that resolved cleanly. The honest pulse should be short.

repo:acme/payments.content:
PR #482: Add idempotency-key support to the /charges endpoint

Implements a Redis-backed dedupe layer keyed on the client-provided Idempotency-Key header. Ships with integration tests covering the replay path and expiration window.
Review on #482 (approved): Nice factoring of the dedupe store interface. One nit on the TTL default — 24h feels short if a client retries after an overnight outage. Worth discussing before merge.
Review on #482 (commented): Agreed with Marcus on the TTL. Also: can we add a metric for dedupe hit rate so we can see how often this fires in prod?

PR #484: Bump protobuf to 4.25.3 to pick up CVE-2024-7254 fix

Mechanical dependency bump. CI is green. No code changes.

slack:eng-backend.content: #eng-backend: heads up, the staging canary for the idempotency PR is running hot on Redis — looks like the TTL sweep is more expensive than we thought. Digging in.
#eng-backend (thread): thanks Jamie — I switched the sweep to a scan-based pass in commit abc123 and canary is back to baseline.
#eng-backend: merging the protobuf bump now that security signed off. Will watch the deploy.

slack:prod-alerts.content: #prod-alerts: elevated 5xx on the webhook dispatcher, up from 0.02% to 0.8% over the last 15 min. I'm on it.
#prod-alerts (thread): root cause was a downstream rate-limit from Stripe on webhook forwarding. Added backoff-and-jitter, rate is back to normal. Will write up the postmortem tomorrow.
#prod-alerts: postmortem for the webhook 5xx incident is up. TL;DR: we weren't respecting Stripe's Retry-After header. Action items in the doc.
